summarySeed.oneLiner: This page directly compares tier-1 AI video generation models across speed, resolution, realism, pricing, and input flexibility. Tools are grouped by market position: frontier models pushing the boundaries of fidelity and capabilities, and creator-access tools that are more accessible to working creators and smaller teams. Single-product reviews are excluded — this page focuses entirely on head-to-head model comparison.
boundaries: Contrasts multiple tier-1 models directly against each other.
Evaluates across speed, resolution, realism, API cost, and input support.
Excludes single-product reviews; focuses on cross-model market comparison.
summarySeed.primaryClassificationRule: Group by market position and access model.

howToChooseSeeds.title: Start with your production goal: social speed or cinematic fidelity.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: If you need fast social clips at volume, prioritize Kling 3.0 (native 4K at 60fps with the cheapest API cost) or Pika (beginner-friendly with a free tier). If you need photorealistic, physics-aware B-roll, prioritize Sora (longest native clip at 25 seconds) or Runway (exceptional motion quality).

howToChooseSeeds.title: Check native resolution before committing.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: Not all models generate in 4K. Kling 3.0 offers native 4K (3840×2160) at 60fps. Veo 3.1 supports 1080p to 4K. Sora and Seedance max out at 1080p / 2K for base generations. Resolution requirements should narrow your shortlist early.

howToChooseSeeds.title: Compare multimodal input support if character consistency matters.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: Most models accept only text prompts or single images. Seedance 2.0 is reported to support up to 12-file multimodal input (images, video, audio) in a single request — relevant for workflows that require strict character or style consistency across scenes.

howToChooseSeeds.title: Evaluate API cost per second if building an integration.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: API pricing varies dramatically. Kling is currently the cheapest at roughly $0.029/sec. Veo costs $0.15/sec (fast mode). Sora ranges from $0.10–0.50/sec. For app builders generating at volume, this difference compounds quickly.

groups.title: Frontier models
groups.rule: These are higher-end or closed model systems positioned around maximum fidelity, novel input modes, or cutting-edge capabilities. They represent the current technical frontier of AI video generation — physics-aware rendering, multimodal input, and native audio sync — though they may have limited access or higher cost.

groups.title: Creator-access tools
groups.rule: This group covers generation models that are more accessible to working creators and smaller teams. They typically offer lower entry prices, free tiers, and more intuitive interfaces — trading some raw technical ceiling for practical usability and faster onboarding.

tools.name: Kling 3.0
tools.verdictSeed: Native 4K at 60fps — reported as the first model to reach this resolution. Cheapest API cost at $0.029/sec. 6-cut multi-shot storyboarding support.
tools.policySeed: Access starts with a daily free-credit tier, but current source coverage does not confirm native audio or stronger governance controls.
tools.bestForSeed: High-resolution volume and social media speed

tools.name: Sora
tools.verdictSeed: Photorealistic text-to-video with physics-aware generation. Longest native clip duration at 25 seconds. Includes a storyboard timeline editor.
tools.policySeed: High-resolution Sora output is priced per second, making it a premium generation path rather than a low-cost batch workflow.
tools.mainLimitationSeed: No native audio generation; no free tier

tools.name: Veo 3.1
tools.verdictSeed: Google's generation model with native audio sync and official API access for developers. Supports up to 4K output.
tools.policySeed: Official API access is part of Veo's value proposition, but current source coverage still describes preview access for UI-first users as limited.
tools.bestForSeed: Developer API integration with native audio sync
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Limited preview access for UI-based users; max clip duration is 8 seconds

tools.name: Seedance 2.0
tools.verdictSeed: Supports up to 12-file multimodal input (images, video, audio) in a single generation request. Native audio-video sync.
tools.pricingSeed: 260 free credits on signup; API: $0.10–0.80/min (third-party only)
tools.policySeed: Access currently looks more experimental than polished: free signup credits exist, but API access is described through third-party providers rather than a cleaner first-party path.
tools.bestForSeed: Maximum creative control via multimodal input
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Frame rate locked to 24fps; API available only through third-party providers

tools.name: Runway
tools.verdictSeed: Exceptional motion quality with consistent character coherence. Offers a broader creative suite with professional editing integration and team workflows.
tools.policySeed: Free keeps a watermark, while paid plans remove watermarks and unlock access to all video models.
tools.bestForSeed: Creative teams needing a broader editing suite alongside generation
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Costs more than Kling for high-volume generation if API economics matter more than workflow depth

tools.name: Pika
tools.verdictSeed: Intuitive interface ideal for beginners and social media creators. Free tier available for experimentation.
tools.policySeed: Lower tiers are watermarked and personal-use oriented; Pro and Fancy unlock unwatermarked commercial use.
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Best for fast social clips, not frontier realism or technical-control workflows

faqSeeds.question: What qualifies a tool for AI Video Generators Comparison (2026)?
faqSeeds.answerSeed: Group by market position and access model. Must contrast multiple tier-1 models directly against each other. Must evaluate models across different dimensions (speed, resolution, realism, price). Excludes single-product reviews; focuses entirely on market comparisons.

faqSeeds.question: What should readers compare first on this page?
faqSeeds.answerSeed: Define Your Production Needs: Are you creating fast social clips (prioritize Kling or Luma) or cinematic, story-driven B-roll (prioritize Sora or Runway)? Check Native Resolutions: Not all models generate in 4K. Kling 3.0 offers native 4K at 60fps, while Sora and Veo generally max out at 1080p for base generations.

faqSeeds.question: Which tools already have internal review coverage?
faqSeeds.answerSeed: Current internal review coverage in this normalized dataset: sora, runway, pika.
